response.justification: Wei Chen is a full-stack web and systems software developer with a strong background in TypeScript, Node.js, and distributed services, but has zero overlap with the core requirements of this role. The position demands SoC and IP modeling, deep-learning workload performance analysis, hardware-software co-design, and GPU/SIMD architecture experience — none of which appear anywhere in this resume. The candidate's closest brush with performance work is low-latency HTTP services written in DLang at Sociomantic, which is categorically different from silicon-level AI accelerator optimization. The educational background (BS Computer Science + BA Fine Arts) does not meet the preferred graduate-level engineering credentials for a Principal-level hardware architecture role.
strengths: Long career (16+ years) demonstrating sustained technical growth and adaptability across many platforms and languages
Low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic using async I/O and high-performance databases shows some performance-oriented thinking
Breadth across embedded, systems, and infrastructure contexts suggests ability to work close to hardware compared to a pure web developer
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or any silicon/hardware design experience — the foundational requirement of the role is entirely absent
No AI or deep learning background whatsoever; MCP agent tooling at re:cinq is software infrastructure, not workload optimization
No GPU, SIMD, MIMD, or accelerator architecture exposure at any point in a 16-year career

key_factors.factor: No AI/ML workload or GPU architecture background
